issues.name: Healthcare
issues.description: Public vs private health systems, coverage, and access

issues.name: Housing & Development
issues.description: Affordability, zoning, supply, and development policy

issues.name: Climate & Energy
issues.description: Decarbonization, energy policy, fossil fuels, renewables

issues.name: Law & Order
issues.description: Gun control, criminal justice, policing, sentencing

issues.name: Labour & Workers
issues.description: Union power, wages, working conditions, strikes

issues.name: Social Values
issues.description: Abortion, LGBTQ+ rights, gender identity, religious freedom

issues.name: Foreign Policy & Trade
issues.description: NATO, military spending, international relations, trade

issues.name: Economy & Taxation
issues.description: Corporate taxes, government spending, deficits, fiscal policy

issues.name: Governance & Democracy
issues.description: Federalism, electoral reform, language, media, institutions

factions.name: Social Liberal Wing
factions.description: Progressive on social issues, interventionist on economy, strong public programs

keyPositions.healthcare: Universal public system, pharmacare, dental care

factions.historicalContext: Dominant under Pierre Trudeau (1970s-80s), resurgent under Trudeau Jr.

factions.name: Centrist Pragmatists
factions.description: Balance efficiency with social programs, moderate on most issues, incremental change

keyPositions.healthcare: Public system with private options, modest funding increases
keyPositions.labor: Work with unions, not against them

factions.historicalContext: Dominated Canadian politics 1993-2006, currently strong faction

factions.name: Bay Street Liberals

keyPositions.healthcare: More private sector involvement, user fees

factions.historicalContext: Influential on economic policy, weaker on social issues

factions.name: Civil Libertarian Liberals
factions.description: Strong on civil rights and privacy, skeptical of government power, progressive on freedoms

keyPositions.lgbtq: Full rights expansion, no religious exemptions

factions.historicalContext: Smaller faction, influential on justice/privacy issues

factions.name: Socialist Left
factions.description: Left-wing, anti-capitalist, supports worker control, radical social change

keyPositions.labor: Strong union support, worker control of production
keyPositions.environment: Radical climate action, ban fossil fuels

factions.historicalContext: Present since CCF founding, minority faction within NDP

factions.name: Social Democrats
factions.description: Left-leaning but accepts capitalism, strong public programs, worker power
factions.historicalContext: Dominant faction, provides NDP mainstream appeal

factions.name: Progressive Identity Politics
factions.description: Focus on social justice, identity issues, intersectionality, activism

factions.name: Economic Nationalist NDP
factions.description: Left-wing economically, nationalist on trade/sovereignty, Canadian focus

keyPositions.trade: Skeptical of free trade, protect Canadian industries
keyPositions.resources: Resource nationalism, keep wealth in Canada

factions.historicalContext: Present in Western Canada, less prominent nationally

factions.name: Social Conservative Wing
factions.description: Right-wing economically and socially, traditional values, law and order

keyPositions.lgbtq: Oppose expansion of rights, religious exemptions
keyPositions.law-order: Law and order priority, tough on crime

factions.historicalContext: Influential since Reform/Alliance merger, strong Western support

factions.name: Red Tory / Progressive Conservative
factions.description: Market-oriented but pragmatic, some social spending, moderate on social issues

keyPositions.healthcare: Public system but more efficiency focus
keyPositions.social: Pragmatic on social issues, not ideological

factions.historicalContext: Declining faction, stronger under Progressive Conservatives

factions.name: Libertarian Right
factions.description: Strong free market, minimal government, individual liberty, low taxes
factions.historicalContext: Present since Alliance days, strong in Western Canada

factions.name: Western Populist / Sovereigntist
factions.description: Right-wing economically and socially, strong Canadian/Western nationalism, anti-establishment

keyPositions.sovereignty: Strong Canadian sovereignty, skeptical of globalism

factions.historicalContext: Strong in Western Canada, growing appeal across party

contradictions.name: Housing Affordability vs. Opposition to Density
contradictions.description: Wanting affordable housing while opposing density creates a fundamental tension
contradictions.explanation: Housing affordability is primarily constrained by supply. Density is a major supply lever. Most affordable housing solutions require some density increase. Opposing both makes affordability harder.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Affordability
resolutions.description: Accept density as necessary for affordability
resolutions.text: Support building more apartments and townhouses in urban areas to increase supply and reduce prices. Accept some neighborhood change as the cost of affordability.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Neighborhood Character
resolutions.description: Accept that this means higher housing costs
resolutions.text: Maintain neighborhood character and oppose density. Use other tools for affordability: subsidies, rent control, public housing. Accept that housing will remain expensive.

resolutions.name: Compromise Position
resolutions.description: Support modest density with design standards
resolutions.text: Allow some density increase but with strong urban design standards, transit investment, and community services. The 'missing middle' approach.

contradictions.name: Climate Action vs. Oil Expansion
contradictions.description: Supporting rapid climate action while approving oil sands expansion is fundamentally contradictory
contradictions.explanation: Oil sands expansion increases Canadian emissions. Rapid decarbonization and oil expansion are mutually exclusive. This contradiction reflects the tension between climate goals and energy industry support.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Climate
resolutions.description: Ban oil expansion, transition economy
resolutions.text: Support banning new oil sands projects. Accept economic transition costs. Invest heavily in renewable energy and retraining.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Energy Security
resolutions.description: Accept emissions, manage them with offsets
resolutions.text: Support oil expansion for energy security and jobs. Use carbon pricing and offsets to address emissions. Accept slower transition.

resolutions.name: Compromise Position
resolutions.description: Limited expansion with strict emissions management
resolutions.text: Allow limited, temporary expansion while rapidly transitioning economy. Strict emissions controls on expansion. Heavy investment in clean energy.

contradictions.name: High Immigration + Cheap Housing (Supply Conflict)
contradictions.description: High immigration intake combined with expectation of cheap housing creates supply/demand conflict
contradictions.explanation: Population growth from high immigration increases housing demand. Housing supply is constrained. High immigration + cheap housing requires massive supply increases. This contradiction often appears when not considering supply-side solutions.

resolutions.name: Accept Housing Cost Increases
resolutions.description: High immigration, accept higher housing prices
resolutions.text: Support high immigration but accept that it increases housing prices. This is economically normal. Focus immigration on people with resources.

resolutions.name: Lower Immigration, Protect Affordability
resolutions.description: Reduce immigration to protect housing affordability
resolutions.text: Reduce immigration to match housing supply. Prioritize citizens' housing needs. Lower immigration solves supply-demand mismatch.

resolutions.name: Massive Housing Supply Increase
resolutions.description: Support both with aggressive supply-side policies
resolutions.text: Support high immigration AND upzone aggressively, eliminate zoning restrictions, build public housing. Requires political will for density.

contradictions.name: Strong Worker Protections + Low Prices (Cost Conflict)
contradictions.description: Wanting strong worker protections and union power while expecting low consumer prices
contradictions.explanation: Higher worker pay and protections increase labor costs, which increase prices. Strong unions and cheap goods are partially in tension. This contradiction appears when not considering trade-offs.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Worker Wages
resolutions.description: Accept higher prices for better worker conditions
resolutions.text: Support strong unions and worker power. Accept that this increases prices. Quality of life for workers more important than cheap goods.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Low Prices
resolutions.description: Accept lower wages for consumer savings
resolutions.text: Minimize worker regulations to keep costs low. Accept that this means lower wages. Consumer savings and jobs matter more.

resolutions.name: Efficiency + Fair Wages
resolutions.description: Productivity increases allow both
resolutions.text: Support worker protections but through productivity gains, not regulations. Invest in training and efficiency to pay workers more without raising prices.

contradictions.name: Individual Freedom vs. Drug Enforcement
contradictions.description: Wanting both individual freedom and strict drug enforcement
contradictions.explanation: Strict drug enforcement requires government limiting individual freedom to use drugs. Full freedom and full enforcement are in tension.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Freedom
resolutions.description: Decriminalize drugs, focus on treatment
resolutions.text: Legalize or decriminalize drugs. Treat addiction as health issue, not criminal. Accept risks of more drug use.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Enforcement
resolutions.description: Accept limits on freedom to prevent drug use
resolutions.text: Strong drug enforcement, strict penalties, limit drug access. Accept that this limits some freedoms. Protect public health.

resolutions.name: Treat & Harm Reduction
resolutions.description: Allow use but manage harms
resolutions.text: Decriminalize use, provide treatment and harm reduction. Heavy enforcement on trafficking. Balance freedom with public health.

contradictions.name: Pro-Trade vs. Sovereignty
contradictions.description: Supporting free trade while wanting Canadian sovereignty can create tensions
contradictions.explanation: Free trade agreements limit some national sovereignty by requiring compliance with international rules. Extreme sovereignty limits trade. Some tension exists but is manageable.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Trade
resolutions.description: Accept some sovereignty limits for trade benefits
resolutions.text: Support free trade agreements. Accept that international rules limit some sovereignty options. Trade benefits outweigh sovereignty concerns.

resolutions.name: Prioritize Sovereignty
resolutions.description: Limit trade to protect sovereignty
resolutions.text: Support protectionist policies. Maintain full sovereignty over regulations. Accept higher prices for independence.

resolutions.name: Strategic Trade
resolutions.description: Trade where beneficial, protect key sectors
resolutions.text: Selective trade agreements that preserve sovereignty on critical sectors. Protect domestic industries while accessing markets.

contradictions.name: Want Services but Oppose Higher Taxes
contradictions.description: Wanting government services while opposing tax increases
contradictions.explanation: Government services cost money. Wanting more services but lower taxes requires either cutting other services or running deficits. This is unsustainable long-term.

resolutions.name: Accept Higher Taxes
resolutions.description: Pay for services through taxation
resolutions.text: Support higher taxes to fund desired services. More progressive taxation funds redistribution and services.

resolutions.name: Reduce Services
resolutions.description: Keep taxes low by cutting services
resolutions.text: Accept lower service levels to keep taxes low. Cut government programs. Limited government model.

resolutions.name: Efficiency Model
resolutions.description: Same services, lower costs through efficiency
resolutions.text: Maintain services but improve government efficiency. Technology, better management reduce costs. Same services, lower taxes.
